page: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is a key component of this server, offering 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a 75 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions and encryption, as well as Geekbench ray tracing and image processing. Multi-core performance is robust, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching 338.82 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 132.78 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, falls within the average range.

Given its bare-metal architecture, high-density local NVMe storage, and large memory capacity, the i4i.metal offers high resource density for demanding enterprise workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, transactional systems, data warehousing, and high-throughput Redis caching layers, as demonstrated by its strong database operations and Redis SET benchmarks. The lack of integrated GPU hardware makes it less suitable for direct machine learning acceleration, but its massive storage and high network bandwidth make it an efficient choice for data-intensive processing and storage-bound applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a substantial 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, backed by a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in CPU encryption, ray tracing, and image processing, alongside strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities. Single-core performance remains average. With its high-density local storage and robust multi-threaded capabilities, the i4i.metal is designed for data-intensive workloads, including large-scale relational databases, NoSQL databases, transactional systems, and high-throughput caching applications.
